explicit_text: My name is Nancy Fong, also known by my Chinese name Kong Y Yee. I was born in Kowloon, Hong Kong, and moved to the United States with my family in 1962 when I was almost five years old. Growing up in Chicago Chinatown, I experienced the challenges of adapting to a new life while my parents worked hard to support us. My story is deeply connected to my family's journey and the sacrifices they made for a better future.

explicit_text: I was born in Kowloon, Hong Kong, where I spent my early childhood surrounded by my family, including my grandmother who doted on me. Life there was different from what I experienced after moving to the United States in 1962. Kowloon was my original home before my family decided to seek better opportunities abroad due to the lack of jobs in Hong Kong at that time.

explicit_text: Hong Kong was where I was born and spent my early years before my family moved to the United States in 1962. Life there was tough because jobs were scarce, which motivated my father to go ahead to the U.S. in 1960 to find work. My childhood memories of Hong Kong include being cared for by my grandmother and having my siblings close by, which changed after we immigrated.

explicit_text: My family and I moved to the United States in 1962 to reunite with my father, who had arrived two years earlier in 1960 seeking work. We settled in Chicago, where I grew up in Chinatown. Life here was challenging at first because my parents worked hard in low-wage jobs, and my mother struggled to learn English on her own. Despite these hardships, the U.S. represented hope and a chance for a better life for my family.

explicit_text: After arriving in the United States, my family settled in Chicago, specifically in the Chinatown neighborhood, where I grew up. We lived on the south side of Chicago, and adjusting to life there was difficult for my parents and siblings. My father worked continuously, and my mother took any job she could find, including factory work. Chicago became my new home where my family worked hard to build a life together after migrating from Hong Kong.
